The scenes are associated with release years 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015 which correspond to decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. The director must ensure no two consecutive scenes come from the same decade. The shooting days for the scenes are 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days. To minimize the total shooting days, we must arrange the scenes such that the sum of days is minimized while satisfying the decade constraint. One optimal sequence is 1994 (4 days), 2004 (6 days), 2012 (10 days), 2007 (8 days), 2015 (12 days). This sequence alternates decades and gives a total of 4+6+10+8+12=40 days.
******
The director has a budget of ₹50 lakhs and scenes with costs per day of ₹1, ₹1.5, ₹2, ₹2.5, and ₹3 lakhs. The goal is to maximize the number of scenes completed within the budget. A linear programming model can be formulated with variables representing the number of days each scene is shot. Constraints ensure the total cost does not exceed ₹50 lakhs. The optimal solution involves selecting scenes with lower costs first to maximize the number of completed scenes. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is 4. Another solution involves prioritizing scenes with higher costs but fewer days to maximize the number of scenes. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is also 4.
